etymology.description: semantic 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") + phonetic 次(次) (cì) -> to sigh
etymology.interpretation: The component 口(口) (kǒu, "mouth") suggests the action of speaking or uttering a sound, while 次(次) (cì) provides the phonetic cue. The combination creates an image of expressing a sound or sigh through the mouth.

examples.chinese: 她轻轻地咨叹了一声(她輕輕地咨嘆了一聲)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīngqīng de zī tàn le yī shēng.

examples.chinese: 想到这里(想到這裡)，他忍不住咨了口气(他忍不住咨了口氣)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎngdào zhèlǐ, tā rěn bú zhù zīle kǒu qì.
examples.english: Thinking of this, he couldn't help but sigh.

examples.chinese: 每当烦恼袭来(每當煩惱襲來)，我总是咨一声(我總是咨一聲)。
examples.pinyin: Měi dāng fánnǎo xílái, wǒ zǒng shì zī yī shēng.
examples.english: Whenever worries arise, I always let out a sigh.
